notes: Baseline for each eval is a no-skill run of the same prompt. A scaffold is 'done' when both launchers dry-run green. Assertions check the five pillars (experiments-as-code, idempotency, in-progress dedup, out-of-workdir artifacts, code staging) are present in generated code and that artifacts are placed at absolute out-of-workdir paths.

evals.name: new-repo-full-scaffold
evals.prompt: Set up MLSpace training + eval launchers in my new repo at ~/proj/mini-sft (a tiny SFT project with scripts/train.py). I want experiments described as code, and artifacts placed OUTSIDE the working directory referenced by absolute paths. Discover which instance types my allocation has. Show me a --dry run at the end.
evals.expected_output: experiments.py (dataclass + collect_experiments + >=1 example), run_train_jobs.py, run_eval.py created in the target repo with an absolute out-of-workdir ARTIFACTS_ROOT and STAGING_ROOT; instance types discovered via `mls job instance_types`; both launchers produce a green --dry run printing payloads without launching.

assertions.text: Created experiments.py with an ExperimentConfig dataclass and a collect_experiments() registry containing at least one concrete experiment
Artifacts root and staging root are ABSOLUTE paths OUTSIDE the target repo working directory (not relative, not under the repo)
Launchers import job helpers from the shared mls library (e.g. training_job_api_from_profile / get_in_progress_jobs / run_job_with_retry) rather than reimplementing API calls
Ran `mls job instance_types` (or clearly used its output) to pick the instance-type map for the allocation
Ran run_train_jobs.py --dry (and/or run_eval.py --dry) and it printed a payload/description without submitting a real job
Confirmed the artifacts root / paths with the user before writing them into code

evals.name: idempotent-dedup-parity
evals.prompt: Add a run_train_jobs.py + experiments.py to this repo that launches on MLSpace following the proven pattern: idempotent (skip experiments whose artifacts already exist) and with in-progress dedup (don't resubmit a job that's already Pending/Running). One job per experiment.
evals.expected_output: Launcher that skips experiments with existing terminal artifacts unless --force, and skips submission when a normalized job description already appears among in-progress jobs from mls; experiments-as-code registry present.

assertions.text: Generated launcher skips experiments whose output artifacts already exist, gated by a --force override
Generated launcher lists in-progress jobs via mls and skips submitting when a normalized job description already matches a Pending/Running job
Job description is normalized (strips #tags/@mentions) before the dedup comparison
experiments.py exposes experiments as code via a dataclass + collect_experiments() registry
Refuses to submit when the git working tree is dirty (explains that staging materializes from .git)

evals.name: code-staging-isolation
evals.prompt: I have a train.py in this repo. Wire up MLSpace job submission so that my later edits to the working tree can't change what an already-launched job runs (clone the code to a separate directory per commit). Then show me a --dry run.
evals.expected_output: Launcher stages the repo to an absolute per-commit directory (cp .git + git reset --hard HEAD) before submitting, refuses dirty trees, points the job's WORKDIR at the staged dir, and dry-runs green.

assertions.text: Launcher stages the repo into an absolute per-commit directory (e.g. <STAGING_ROOT>/<commit_hash>) using a git-based clone (cp .git then git reset --hard HEAD)
Staging is idempotent (reused if present) and the job's WORKDIR/cd points at the staged directory, not the live checkout
Refuses to submit when tracked files are dirty, with a message explaining uncommitted changes would be dropped
Ran the launcher with --dry and it printed the staged-workdir-based script without launching a real job
The --dry output does not leak secrets: HF_TOKEN (and other *_TOKEN/*_KEY/*_SECRET env values) are masked/redacted, not printed verbatim
--dry has no filesystem side effects (does not write experiment_config.yaml or other run artifacts in dry mode)
